name: Provident Bank
description_short: Regional bank serving NJ, NYC, and PA with checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and business banking. FDIC-insured with digital banking and commercial lending services.
description_long:
Provident Bank is a regional financial institution operating across New Jersey, New York City, and Pennsylvania. The bank appears to be an established community bank with FDIC insurance backing, indicating deposits are protected by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The institution maintains both personal and business banking divisions with physical branch locations and modern digital capabilities.

Provident Bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business financial products. On the personal side, they provide multiple checking account tiers (Basic, Cash Back, Platinum, Prestige Suite, Senior, Smart), savings accounts, Money Market accounts, CDs, and IRAs. Lending products include mortgages, home equity loans, personal loans, student loans, and credit cards. Their business services encompass commercial checking and savings, business money market accounts, commercial loans (including real estate and construction), treasury management, and specialized services like ACH origination, positive pay, and remote deposit capture.

Provident Bank distinguishes itself through robust digital banking infrastructure, offering ProvidentConnect online banking for both personal and business customers, mobile banking apps, and integration with major digital payment platforms (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, Zelle). They provide specialized business services including IOLTA accounts for law firms, municipal/government accounts, and treasury management tools. The bank also offers investment services through Provident Investment Services and Beacon Trust, though these are not FDIC-insured products.

As a regional bank, Provident Bank serves customers who prioritize local branch access and community banking relationships alongside digital convenience. However, without disclosed interest rates, promotional offers details, or fee structures on the website, customers cannot easily compare their competitiveness to larger national banks or online-only institutions. The emphasis on business services and specialized lending suggests they compete more heavily in commercial banking than consumer segments. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (multiple tiers: Basic, Cash Back, Platinum, Prestige, Senior, Smart)
Savings accounts and Money Market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and IRA accounts
Commercial checking, savings, and business money market accounts
Commercial loans including real estate, construction, and investment real estate lending
Treasury management and ACH origination services
Remote deposit capture and positive pay
Credit cards (personal and business Mastercard debit cards)
Digital banking (ProvidentConnect, mobile banking, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, Zelle)
Investment services through Provident Investment Services and Beacon Trust
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Multiple checking account options tailored to different customer needs (Senior, Student, Prestige, Cash Back variants)
Comprehensive digital banking including ProvidentConnect, mobile apps, and integration with Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, and Zelle
Strong commercial banking services including treasury management, ACH origination, remote deposit capture, and specialized accounts (IOLTA, municipal)
Diverse lending products spanning personal loans, mortgages, home equity, construction loans, and commercial real estate
Physical branch network across NJ, NYC, and PA for in-person service
Investment and wealth management services through affiliated Provident Investment Services and Beacon Trust
cons: Website does not display interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules, making it difficult to compare competitiveness
No information about minimum account balances, overdraft fees, or other banking costs disclosed
Limited transparency about loan terms, rates, or approval requirements for personal loans, mortgages, and business lending
Investment and wealth management products explicitly noted as not FDIC-insured and may lose value
Website lacks customer reviews, ratings, or transparent disclosure of service standards
best_for: Regional customers in NJ, NYC, and PA seeking FDIC-insured checking and savings with branch access
Small business owners needing comprehensive commercial banking, payroll, and treasury management services
Customers preferring digital-first banking with traditional bank backing and multiple payment platform integration
Borrowers seeking relationship-based lending for mortgages, home equity, or commercial real estate within the tri-state area
diagnosis: Provident Bank is best for regional customers across NJ, NYC, and PA who value FDIC-insured deposits, branch access, and digital banking convenience, as well as small business owners needing comprehensive commercial banking services. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing information on the website—potential customers cannot easily compare interest rates, fees, or loan terms without contacting the bank directly, limiting ability to assess competitiveness against larger national banks or digital-only alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
